columns.downstream_use: Account identification — lead sheets, classification, exception attribution
Balance verification, exception detection, anomaly aggregation
Balance verification, exception detection, anomaly aggregation

issues.remediation: Verify detected columns are correct before proceeding.
issues.downstream_impact: All diagnostic tools depend on correct column mapping. Incorrect detection causes miscalculated balances, failed ratio analysis, and unreliable anomaly detection across every downstream report.

issues.message:
Balance check skipped — one or more balance columns are unmapped. This file appears to use a multi-column layout (unmapped balance-like columns: 'Adjustments
Debit', 'Adjustments
Credit', 'Ending Balance
Debit', 'Ending Balance
Credit'). Map all balance columns before running balance verification.
issues.remediation: Use column mapping to explicitly assign the intended Debit and Credit columns, or re-export the trial balance as a single Debit/Credit pair before re-uploading.
issues.downstream_impact: An out-of-balance trial balance invalidates all downstream diagnostic results. Debit/credit totals must reconcile before any testing can proceed.

issues.message:
Column 'Beg Balance
Credit' has 2 rows with missing data (4.9% of rows)
issues.remediation:
Review rows with missing 'Beg Balance
Credit' values. Fill in missing data or remove incomplete rows before analysis.
issues.downstream_impact: Missing values in critical columns produce incomplete trial balance totals, skewed ratio calculations, and may cause accounts to be silently excluded from testing tools.

issues.message: 2 rows have zero balance in both debit and credit columns (5% of rows)
issues.remediation: Zero-balance rows add noise to the analysis. Consider filtering them out if they represent closed or inactive accounts.
issues.downstream_impact: Zero-balance rows add noise to statistical tests (Benford's Law, sampling), inflate account counts, and reduce the signal-to-noise ratio in anomaly detection.
